text:
*[Bug 19047] Loading a malicious .rds file can crash R*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19047>

Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |ikrylov@disroot.org

--- Comment #1 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
Dear Bill Denney,

Thank you for the reports! Some of them (e.g. MEM-005) are reproducible and
worth fixing. (On POSIX this stack overflow is recoverable, but on Windows it's
a crash.) I could not reproduce MEM-006 or MEM-007 on R-devel/Linux. The bug
report is for R-devel (trunk). What was the version of R that you gave Claude
to review?

In Bug 19015 Comment 1 you mention that you did not review the AI-generated
patch because you are not a C coder. In general, [it's best not to submit
unreviewed bug reports and
patches](<https://daniel.haxx.se/blog/2025/07/14/death-by-a-thousand-slops/>),
whether they come from a machine learning model or not. Here, Claude didn't
notice that `MAXELTSIZE` is not a general-purpose vector size limit in R (it's
only 8192, for small buffers) and that `allocVector()` contains a check for the
(signed) allocation length being negative. Suggested change for `case
BUILTINSXP:` is not covered in the patch description and misses the fact that
`R_CheckStack2` already contains a length check.

MEM-006 is likely invalid because a vector length of `-1` is used to mark a
long vector; `-1` should be followed by two 4-byte integers signifying the real
length up to `R_XLEN_T_MAX` (see the `ReadLENGTH` function). There are only two
lines containing `length = InInteger(stream)` in current R-devel, and they are
both followed by checks on `length`.

MEM-007 is likely invalid as well. While it's true that `ReadBC()` calls
`allocVector(VECSXP, InInteger(stream))` (there's no line containing
`allocVector(VECSXP, nreps)` as quoted by Claude in current R-devel) without
further checks on the desired length, `allocVector()` does check its argument
for being negative.

When writing on Bugzilla, please make use of the Preview tab. Bugzilla speaks
Markdown, but does not understand the GitHub syntax extensions such as
language-marked code blocks (` ```c `). Don't process untrusted serialized data
objects in R, [they are dangerous enough without bugs in
`unserialize()`](<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gDBbRZXKx1s>).

text:
*[Bug 19033] text Chinese with srt gives larger size of character*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19033>

--- Comment #10 from Jinsong Zhao (<mailto:jszhao@yeah.net|jszhao@yeah.net>) ---
`CairoWin()` can display Chinese and possibly other multibyte characters, but
only if a font family that contains the specific character is set.
text

```
library(Cairo)  
CairoWin()  
par(family = "sans")  # This must be included, because the default family is "" 
plot(1, 1, type = "n")  
text(1, 1, '\u4e2d\u6587', pos = 4, srt = 45)  
```

I could not figure out how to display Chinese in `png(..., type = "cairo")`;
however, I found that I could use `Cairo::CairoPNG(...)` instead.

text:
*[Bug 19046] Reading a specially-crafted Rdata file can crash R*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19046>

Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |ikrylov@disroot.org
     Ever confirmed|0                           |1
             Status|UNCONFIRMED                 |CONFIRMED

--- Comment #1 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
The reproducer for MEM-002 is invalid because the buffer is already allocated
by the time a negative-length string is read. The crash will only happen if the
first symbol in the file has a negative length:

```
writeLines(c(
'RDA1',
'1 0', # one symbol
'-1 x' # negative length
), f <- tempfile())
load(f)
```

The reproducer for MEM-033 actually triggers a bug in `InStringXdr`, not
`InStringBinary`, so there's three bugs in total. In `InStringXdr`,
`realloc(buf, nbytes + 1 /* (unsigned)-1 + 1 => 0 */)` frees `buf` and signals
an error because `realloc()` returns `NULL`, so the next call to `load()`
results in a use-after-free.

The analysis for `InStringBinary()` is correct:

```
con <- file(f <- tempfile(), 'wb')
writeChar('RDB1\n', con, eos = NULL)
writeBin(c(1L, 0L), con) # one symbol
writeBin(-1L, con) # negative length
writeChar('a', con, eos = NULL) # enough not to cause EOF on next read
close(con)
load(f)
```

text:
*[Bug 19048] textConnection can cause crash with large strings*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19048>

Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |ikrylov@disroot.org
     Ever confirmed|0                           |1
             Status|UNCONFIRMED                 |CONFIRMED

--- Comment #1 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
The problem is due to `scan()`, not `textConnection()`. The signed integer
overflows are, of course, worth fixing. It's probably better to make one last
try with a buffer of size exactly `INT_MAX` in order to be able to `scan()`
strings between 1 and 2 GiB in size. On a non-sanitized build of R,
`R_AllocStringBuffer()` handles the failing allocation and signals an error
without crashing (although not without leaking memory when assigning the result
of `realloc()`).

Despite what Claude wrote in the comment, there are no calls to `R_Realloc(buf,
nbuf, char)` anywhere in R-devel. Closest I was able to find was `buf =
R_Realloc(buf, need , char);` in `src/main/agrep.c`, which indeed contains an
overflow bug, but Claude didn't say anything about `adist()` in this report.

*[Bug 18288] Add support for SHA2 (256-bit) hashing*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18288>

--- Comment #15 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
`tools::sha256sum()` appeared in R-4.5.0. SHA-256 hashing for packages with
signature verification via GnuPG is present in the 4.6.0 beta. I think this
issue can be considered fixed.

elements.text:
appeared in R-4.5.0. SHA-256 hashing for packages with
signature verification via GnuPG is present in the 4.6.0 beta. I think this
issue can be considered fixed.

text:
*[Bug 16722] Wishlist: API access to connections*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=16722>

Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |ikrylov@disroot.org

--- Comment #4 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
In the 4.6.0 beta version and current R-devel, `tools:::nonAPI` no longer
contains `R_new_custom_connection`, `R_ReadConnection`, `R_WriteConnection`,
and `R_GetConnection`; WRE and `tools:::funAPI()` list these functions as
experimental. I think this issue can be considered fixed.

text:
*[Bug 19047] Loading a malicious .rds file can crash R*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19047>

--- Comment #2 from Bill Denney (<mailto:bill.r-project@denney.ws|bill.r-project@denney.ws>) ---
Thank you for the review. I had Claude use the current subversion source for R
devel, and it was running in Linux.

I apologize for any noise that this generated; I thought that I had verified
all of the bugs actually crashed R, and I did tell it to filter out minor
issues that did not cause memory corruption. (I reported several bugs last
night, but I filtered out about 40 more that were more minor issues to avoid
being the guy who is overloading the system with nuisance bugs.

I am also aware that you shouldn't load untrusted R saved files, but I'm also
confident that not everyone knows that. :)

text:
*[Bug 19039] Error in bytecode that does not appear in interpreter*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19039>

Luke Tierney (<mailto:luke@stat.uiowa.edu|luke@stat.uiowa.edu>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
             Status|CONFIRMED                   |CLOSED
                 CC|                            |luke@stat.uiowa.edu
         Resolution|---                         |INVALID

--- Comment #2 from Luke Tierney (<mailto:luke@stat.uiowa.edu|luke@stat.uiowa.edu>) ---
Replacement function calls as part of complex assignment do not behave like
ordinary function calls. For one, the LHS value will have been evaluated before
the call. `substitute()` returns a symbol, but not one that is intended to be
evaluated or guaranteed to have a value. The way compiled code works is much
less brittle than the way the interpreter works. Eventually the interpreted
code will be brought in line with the compiled code, but it isn't a high
priority.
